chain.thought: Explanation: Considering seasons after 2000–01 (from 2001–02 through 2013–14) and counting postseason results that indicate a late-stage appearance (quarterfinalist, semifinalist, finalist, or champion), they achieved such spots in the following seasons: 2001–02 (quarterfinalist), 2002–03 (finalist), 2003–04 (finalist), 2004–05 (champion), 2005–06 (semifinalist), 2006–07 (champion), 2007–08 (quarterfinalist), 2008–09 (semifinalist), 2009–10 (champion), 2010–11 (champion), 2011–12 (champion), and 2012–13 (champion). Only 2013–14 lacks a postseason result. This totals 12 times.

judge_thought:
Explanation:
"After 2000–01" refers to seasons starting from 2001–02 onward. Interpreting "late-stage spot" as reaching at least the quarterfinals in the postseason (quarterfinalist, semifinalist, finalist, champion), the seasons 2001–02 through 2012–13 (rows 11–22) all show such outcomes:
- 2001–02: quarterfinalist
- 2002–03: finalist
- 2003–04: finalist
- 2004–05: champion
- 2005–06: semifinalist
- 2006–07: champion
- 2007–08: quarterfinalist
- 2008–09: semifinalist
- 2009–10: champion
- 2010–11: champion
- 2011–12: champion
- 2012–13: champion
The 2013–14 postseason entry is blank, so it isn’t counted. That yields a total of 12 times.
